title: peltapteris flabellata
intro: Peltapteris flabellata, also known as the fan-leaved Peltapteris, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a woody vine native to tropical Africa, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Peltapteris flabellata contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, for use in traditional medicine.
